title: activity — cache keys
description: Cache keys this module owns. Retention-policy resolution is cached per-tenant to avoid hitting the DB on every purge job iteration; log-name catalog cached at boot; batch tracking cached per-request; feed cursor cached to detect duplicate broadcast attempts.

keys.description: Resolved retention_days for (tenant, log_name). Reads: PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob per iteration + observer on retention-policy CRUD. Writes: on policy Create/Update/Delete via InvalidateRetentionCache listener. Miss: falls through to DB, then to config default (90d).

Cached copy of `data/log-names-catalog.json` seeded at boot. Consumed by `valid_log_name` rule + LogsActivity trait boot-time validation. Refreshed on `config:cache` clear or on manual `activity:refresh-catalog` command.
keys.note: Un-prefixed — the log-name catalog is platform-wide (registered feeds are shared across every tenant).

keys.description: Per-request batch tracker. Populated by ActivityBatcher.beginBatch() with the batch_uuid + start timestamp + tenant/application/actor. Read by every LogsActivity write to attach the correct batch_uuid. Cleared on request terminate.

Last-broadcast activity id per (tenant, channel). Prevents duplicate broadcasts when the same event fires from multiple workers (Octane pool). The publisher compares the incoming activity.id to the cursor; skips if already broadcast.
keys.note: Short TTL — the cursor doesn't need long-term persistence; it just prevents duplicates within the current broadcast burst.

keys.description: For tenants without `activity_broadcast`, tracks the last poll timestamp per user. Read by /api/v1/activities?filter[from]=<since> when the frontend is polling. Prevents 'give me everything since epoch' abuse on cold-start.

Cached RBAC decision — can this user see activity for this specific (subject_type, subject_id)? Consumed by `activity.enforce_scope` middleware. Fed by AccessGrantResolver's own cache; invalidated on grant lifecycle events.
invalidatedBy: grants::GrantIssued / Revoked (cascades from grants:eval cache invalidation)

invalidationRules.action: Invalidate `activity:retention:{tenant_id}:{log_name}` for the affected pair (or all log_names when log_name IS NULL on the policy).
invalidationRules.reasoning: Retention policy is read on every purge iteration; a stale cache means rows purge on the old ceiling for up to TTL seconds after a change. TTL is 1h — long enough to avoid churn, short enough that even without explicit invalidation the wrong purge would recover within an hour.

invalidationRules.action: Invalidate every activity:* cache key for the tenant. Handled by the PurgeActivitiesForErasedTenant listener as part of its cascade.
invalidationRules.reasoning: Post-erasure, no read should hit stale data — every cache entry for the tenant is dropped explicitly.

invalidationRules.action: Log-name catalog + retention default cache both refreshed. Handled by the ActivityServiceProvider@boot warm-up.
invalidationRules.reasoning: Config changes may alter the module's fallback defaults — cached values must reload.
